summary.intent: Fix a failing CI coverage check on PR #698 where `ars-components` branch coverage had dropped below the 90% threshold due to newly added color component code.
summary.outcome: Branch coverage for `ars-components` was raised from 89.7% to 90.4% (passing) by adding both-arms attribute tests across 6 color modules, fixing a dead-code bug in ColorField (`SetHasDescription`), and updating the spec to match.

repo: CI coverage uses `cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace` (default features). The `--workspace` flag means other crates' tests also exercise `ars-components`, so a per-crate `-p` run always reads lower than CI's number.
`check-all` reads per-crate branch/line percentages from the merged lcov and enforces a 90% branch threshold for `ars-components`.
Adapter crates fail `check-all` on the native lcov because CI merges their wasm coverage separately â€” those failures are expected and unrelated to `ars-components` changes.
Spec-first rule is enforced: new events (e.g., `SetHasDescription`) must be documented in the component's `.md` spec (Â§1.2 events table + Â§1.5 machine transitions) before committing.
`--branch` flag for llvm-cov requires the nightly toolchain; stable builds silently omit branch data.
The `i18n` feature enables ICU-gated tests but CI does not use it for the native coverage run (default features only).
Historical `ars-components` branch coverage baseline was 92.3%; the new color modules pulled it down ~2.6pp before this fix.

code.finding: ColorField was missing the `SetHasDescription` event handler that the sibling `field` component has. The `has_description` flag was never set to true, making the `aria-describedby` branch permanently dead. Fixed by adding the event and a corresponding test.
Loop variable named `key` shadowed the `key()` helper function, causing a compile error. Renamed the loop variable to fix.
`all_parts` is an array of non-`Copy` `Part` values; the first loop moved the array, breaking subsequent iterations. Fixed by iterating by reference and cloning.

`snap_to_step` (stepâ‰¤0 guard) and `wrap_value` (rangeâ‰¤0 guard) defensive branches are coverable with edge-case inputs, unlike the `(max-min).abs() > EPSILON` divide-guards in color channel math which are structurally unreachable.

workflow: To replicate CI's branch coverage number locally, run `cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace` and then `cargo xtask check-all` â€” the per-crate `-p` shortcut underreports.

Pattern for covering one-armed `if flag { ... }` conditionals: write a 'both arms' test that exercises the component with the flag false (idle state) and then true (active state) in the same test function.
Before writing tests, run per-module branch summaries to rank components by miss count and prioritize the worst offenders first.

Distinguish coverable defensive branches (edge inputs, unusual-but-valid states) from structurally dead ones (mathematical invariants that channel types guarantee never trigger) â€” only add tests for the former.
After fixing coverage in a subset of modules, project the crate-wide improvement before running the slow workspace measurement: (new_covered_branches / total_branches) gives a reliable pre-check.

friction: `--branch` requiring nightly was not obvious upfront; the first coverage attempt silently ran without branch data before the toolchain check.
`--show-missing-lines` dumps the entire crate regardless of test filter, making it hard to isolate missed lines for a specific module.

Local per-crate run (89.28%) vs CI workspace run (89.7%) discrepancy caused confusion about whether changes were sufficient â€” required reading the CI orchestration code to understand the scope difference.
The `all_parts` move error was only caught at compile time during the test run, not during the initial edit.
open_items: Divide-guard branches (`(max-min).abs() > EPSILON`) in color channel math are intentionally left uncovered â€” they are structural invariants guaranteed by channel type constraints, not reachable with real inputs.
Branch coverage margin is thin (~90.4% vs 90% threshold); future additions to color modules risk regression without additional test discipline.
The `check-all` failure on adapter crates in the native lcov is expected (wasm coverage merged separately by CI) but could confuse future contributors running the command locally.
